mandate: Pivot SMS routing from per-couple Twilio number lookup to guest-phone-based couple resolution. All beta couples share one Twilio number.
keyFindings: The UNIQUE constraint on phone_numbers.twilio_number was named phone_numbers_twilio_number_key (Postgres default naming convention)
The guests table already has an indexed phone column (idx_guests_phone) making guest-based lookup efficient
The Inngest sms-received function receives coupleId in its event payload and does not perform its own phone number lookup — no changes needed there
The provision-number route hardcodes the shared number and already has idempotency checks per couple — works correctly with the UNIQUE constraint dropped
whatWorked: Reading the full flow (webhook -> Inngest -> reply) before making changes confirmed the Inngest function was already decoupled from the routing decision. Only the webhook needed changes.
whatFailed: Nothing failed. The change was clean.
recommendationsForNextAgent: The multi-couple edge case (guest registered with multiple couples) uses conversation recency as tiebreaker. If this becomes common, consider a UI for guests to select which couple they want to message, or route based on the most recent couple who added them.
spiritContribution: Enabled the shared-number beta model which removes the per-couple Twilio number cost barrier, directly serving the September launch goal by making it cheaper to onboard the first 10-12 couples.
